name: Epirus
description: High-power microwave directed energy systems for counter-drone defense. Leonidas system disables drone swarms with focused microwave energy. $550M+ raised, major Army and DoD contracts.
insight: Directed energy is the only cost-effective answer to drone swarms — you can't spend a $1M missile on a $500 drone. Epirus's Leonidas changes the economics of air defense fundamentally.

name: Hermeus
description: Hypersonic aircraft company — $500M Series C at $1B valuation April 2026; Quarterhorse uncrewed hypersonic testbed with DoD contracts. HQ moved from Atlanta to Los Angeles April 2026.
insight: Hermeus's military-first strategy is smart — DoD will pay for Mach 5 capabilities that commercial aviation won't need for decades. The Chimera engine's turbine-to-ramjet transition is the key technical risk.

name: Radiant
description: Portable 1.2MW nuclear microreactors for austere environments and off-grid use. $300M Series D. Selected for DOE Reactor Pilot Program. 20-reactor Equinix pre-order. Air Force deployment by 2028.
insight: Radiant's portable microreactor could be the most important energy product of the decade for defense. 1.2MW that fits on a truck and powers a forward operating base without fuel convoys — that's a game-changer.

name: Saildrone
description: Autonomous wind and solar-powered ocean drones for maritime ISR, surveillance, ocean data collection, and climate monitoring. Operates the world's largest fleet of uncrewed surface vehicles.

name: Sift
description: Telemetry and observability stack for mission-critical machines ('Datadog for physical systems') — Series B ~$42M 2025 at $274M post-money with major space/defense customers.
insight: Every rocket, satellite, and factory needs real-time telemetry. Sift is the first horizontal platform that does it.

name: Physical Intelligence
founder: Karol Hausman, Sergey Levine, Chelsea Finn, Brian Ichter
description: AI foundation model for robotics. Building general-purpose robot intelligence that enables physical manipulation and interaction. $600M Series B at $5.6B valuation. Major Bay Area robotics lab backed
insight: Physical Intelligence is the leading contender for building the 'GPT of robots.' Their approach — a single generalist model controlling diverse robot hardware — could become the default AI layer for the entire robotics industry.

name: Boom Supersonic
founder: Blake Scholl, Joe Wilding, Josh Krall
description: Designing and building the Overture supersonic commercial jet. Created the XB-1 demonstrator which completed test flights. Designing proprietary Symphony engine. 130+ orders from airlines including Un
insight: Boom is betting that proprietary engines (Symphony) are the unlock — no one else is building a purpose-built supersonic commercial engine. 130+ airline orders suggest real demand if they can deliver on certification.

name: Cognition
founder: Scott Wu, Steven Hao, Walden Yan
description: Building Devin, the world's first AI software engineer. Autonomous coding agent that can plan, write, debug, and deploy software end-to-end.
insight: Devin represents the frontier of AI agents — not just generating code but planning, debugging, and deploying autonomously. If this works at scale, every software team becomes 10x more productive.

name: Scale AI
description: AI data infrastructure providing high-quality training data for machine learning and defense applications. $29B valuation. Powers AI development for government and enterprise. Youngest self-made billi
insight: Scale AI is the invisible infrastructure layer powering every major AI model. Their defense pivot with Scale Donovan is positioning them as the 'Palantir for AI data.' At $29B, they're priced for category dominance.

name: Bedrock Robotics
description: Retrofits existing heavy construction equipment — excavators, bulldozers, loaders — with reversible hardware and software to enable fully autonomous operations on job sites. Founded by former Waymo en
insight: Reached $1.75B valuation in under two years from founding. The retrofit approach is genius — why replace a $500K excavator when you can make it autonomous for a fraction of the cost? Targeting an 800,000 worker shortage in construction.

name: Terrahaptix
description: AI-powered surveillance drones for critical infrastructure protection (dba Terra Industries) — $34M from 8VC + Lux Capital; won $1.2M NetHawk contract over an Israeli firm; exports to 8 African countr
insight: Africa's best defense-tech story of 2025 — and it's backed by top US funds. Terra is the continent's first credible hard-tech defense unicorn trajectory.

name: Bear Robotics
description: Service robotics at scale (Servi) — $160M+ raised with SoftBank backing; thousands of robots deployed in restaurants and hotels globally.
insight: Most service robotics startups haven't reached commercial scale. Bear did — with real margin.

name: Saronic
description: Autonomous surface vessels built at shipyard scale — Series D $1.75B at $9.25B valuation March 2026, $2.6B total raised across Series C ($600M) and earlier.
insight: Saronic's 'shipyard as startup' model is revolutionary — they're building autonomous naval vessels on software development timelines, not traditional shipbuilding cycles. The Navy desperately needs this speed.

name: Figure AI
description: General-purpose humanoid robots for industrial applications. $1B+ raised at $39B valuation in latest round. Figure 02 deployed commercially. Backed by Microsoft, OpenAI, NVIDIA, Bezos, Intel. The huma
insight: Figure's $39B valuation makes it the most valuable humanoid robotics company in history. The OpenAI partnership gives them the best AI model integration of any robotics company. Commercial deployments at BMW validate the path.

name: Skild AI
description: Foundation model for robotics — building a general-purpose robot brain that can control any robot for any task. $1.4B raised at $14B+ valuation. The 'GPT moment' for physical intelligence.
insight: Skild's $14B+ valuation makes it one of the most valuable robotics AI companies in the world. The CMU robotics pedigree and massive funding give them runway to compete head-to-head with Physical Intelligence and Figure AI.

name: Fortastra
description: Orbital defense satellites with rendezvous-and-proximity-operations capabilities — $8M seed for space-domain defense.
insight: Next-generation satellite bus platform going after the new space economy. Torrance-based, led by defense veteran Mike Smayda. Early stage but strong fundamentals.

name: Varda Space Industries
description: Manufacturing pharmaceuticals in microgravity and returning them to Earth via re-entry capsules. Launched 5 missions in 2025. $187M Series C from Founders Fund and Khosla Ventures.
insight: Co-founded by Delian Asparouhov from Founders Fund. In-space pharmaceutical manufacturing is the killer app for microgravity — plus hypersonic re-entry capability is the ultimate dual-use technology.

name: Hadrian
description: AI-powered precision manufacturing factories producing aerospace and defense parts for primes like Lockheed — $560M raised at $1.6B valuation; Factory 3 opening in Mesa, AZ.
insight: Hadrian is solving the bottleneck no one talks about: precision manufacturing capacity. While everyone builds the weapons systems, someone has to machine the parts — and US capacity is dangerously thin.

name: GrayMatter Robotics
description: Deploys AI-powered robotic cells using proprietary 'physics-informed AI' for complex surface finishing — sanding, grinding, polishing, spraying — in high-mix manufacturing. Robots self-program from ta
insight: Named Fast Company Most Innovative in Robotics 2025. Their physics-informed AI means robots can handle complex curved surfaces that defeated previous automation attempts. Robot-as-a-Service model lowers adoption barriers for SMBs.

name: Collaborative Robotics
description: Builds 'Proxie,' a mobile manipulator cobot that carries 75 lb payloads and moves carts up to 1,500 lb. Deliberate non-humanoid design optimized for real-world utility. Clients include Mayo Clinic, Ma
insight: Founded by Brad Porter, former Amazon VP of Robotics who led 10,000 people. When the guy who ran Amazon's robot army starts his own company, you pay attention. Backed by Sequoia, General Catalyst, and Khosla — the ultimate signal. Non-humanoid design is a bet that function trumps form.

name: DZYNE Technologies
description: Autonomous aircraft and counter-UAS platform — $80M+ in DoD R&D; acquired High Point Aerotechnologies 2024; $49M AF SBIR; 125K sqft Irvine facility.
insight: DZYNE has been quietly executing for a decade. High Point acquisition and $49M AF SBIR validate the scale-up.

name: Multiply Labs
description: Robotic pharmaceutical manufacturing for cell and gene therapies — UCSF study June 2025 showed 74% CGT cost reduction; Kyverna partnership.
insight: First company to bring 3D printed capsules to market. The $85M partnership with Retro Biosciences for age-related disease therapies validates the platform. NVIDIA Physical AI partnership (Jan 2026) positions them at the intersection of robotics + biotech + AI — a triple threat.

name: Orangewood Labs
founder: Abhinav Das, Aditya Bhatia, Akash Bansal
description: Affordable, AI-powered collaborative robotic arms for small and medium businesses. Uses natural language programming (RoboGPT) to democratize industrial robotics at a fraction of traditional costs.
insight: The 'Tesla of robotic arms' play — making automation affordable enough for the 98% of factories that can't afford a $100K Universal Robots setup. Natural language programming via RoboGPT is a genuinely novel interface. Y Combinator backed. Crossed $1M ARR.

name: Cohere
founder: Aidan Gomez, Ivan Zhang, Nick Frosst
description: Enterprise-focused AI company building custom LLMs for business applications. Founded by Google Brain alumni, including a co-author of the transformative 'Attention Is All You Need' paper.
insight: Cohere is Canada's answer to OpenAI for enterprise. Their focus on privacy and data sovereignty resonates with enterprise buyers wary of sending data to US cloud providers.

name: Castelion
description: Designing and manufacturing hypersonic missiles with rapid iteration. Blackbeard weapon system integrated with US Army and Navy. $350M Series B led by Altimeter and Lightspeed.

name: Cambridge Aerospace
description: Skyhammer jet-powered drone/missile interceptor — won UK MoD contract April 2026 with first deliveries in May.
insight: Counter-drone economics are broken. Cambridge Aerospace is one of very few solutions that cost less than the threats they kill.

name: Pano AI
description: Integrated platform of high-definition panoramic cameras, thermal imaging, and computer vision AI for real-time wildfire smoke detection. Covers nearly 30 million acres, serves 250+ public safety agen
insight: Full-stack approach (hardware + AI + human verification + alerting) differentiates from satellite-only competitors. With California wildfires causing $10B+ in annual damage, Pano's early detection infrastructure is becoming essential public utility. $100M+ in booked revenue shows real demand.

name: Atmos Space Cargo
description: Inflatable reentry capsule 'Phoenix' — first flight April 2025; reusable in-orbit manufacturing downmass provider.
insight: Affordable downmass is the orbital-manufacturing keystone. Atmos's inflatable design is the German answer.

name: Shield AI
founder: Brandon Tseng, Ryan Tseng, Andrew Reiter
description: Hivemind autonomy software that flies aircraft in swarms without GPS. Gary Steele took over as CEO December 2025. Dual-use AI with real-world deterrence value. $5.6B valuation.
insight: Hivemind is the real product — the airframes are just the delivery mechanism. If Shield AI can make any aircraft autonomous in GPS-denied environments, they become the Android of military aviation.

name: GHGSat
description: Operates the world's largest fleet of high-resolution satellites dedicated to methane monitoring. Serves ExxonMobil, Aramco, and UN organizations.
insight: GHGSat is becoming the de facto global standard for methane emissions verification. As carbon markets mature, their data becomes critical infrastructure.

name: Turion Space
description: Space debris removal and real-time orbital domain awareness. Building autonomous spacecraft for on-orbit servicing, proximity operations, and space sustainability.

name: Axiom Space
description: Commercial space station development and government microgravity services — $350M round February 2026; $850M+ total. Navigating post-2024 restructuring with turnaround underway.

name: Aetherflux
description: Space-based solar power using low Earth orbit satellites and infrared laser power beaming. Delivering clean energy from space directly to ground receivers for energy independence.

name: Focused Energy
description: Pursuing laser-driven inertial fusion energy, inspired by the NIF breakthrough. Using advanced laser systems to achieve commercial fusion power.

name: Built Robotics
description: Autonomous AI systems that retrofit heavy construction equipment with sensors and guidance systems to enable autonomous operation of excavators, dozers, and loaders.

name: Endurosat
description: Bulgarian satellite manufacturing company building nanosatellites and providing space services.

name: Ares Industries
description: Next-generation precision-guided munitions and missile systems designed for rapid production and modern warfare requirements.

name: Hadean
description: Distributed-compute platform for military simulation, wargaming, and synthetic training environments.
insight: Live-fire training costs billions and can't match modern war's complexity. Synthetic training has to scale — and Hadean's architecture actually does.

name: Adarga
description: NLP/GenAI intelligence platform fusing unstructured data for defense analysts — up to $14.8M UK MoD contract.
insight: Palantir-scale intelligence platforms are oligopoly today. Adarga + QinetiQ gives UK sovereign access.

name: Blykalla
description: Lead-cooled fast small modular reactor (55 MW Sealer) targeting data centers and industry, co-led by Oklo and Norrsken.
insight: Sweden is quietly becoming a serious SMR exporter. Blykalla has the cleanest lead-cooled design and EU backing.

name: Harmattan AI
description: AI-enabled combat micro-drones (1.8kg, autonomous targeting) — $200M Series B led by Dassault Aviation January 2026 at a $1.4B valuation; DGA contract for 1,000 drones in 2025.
insight: France's DGA rarely anchors startups — they did here. Harmattan is the French equivalent of Anduril's Roadrunner.

name: Raphe mPhibr
description: Heavy-lift and high-altitude military UAVs — $100M June 2025 round, used in Operation Sindoor, part of MoD's ₹30,000 Cr MALE UAV order.
insight: India needs sovereign UAVs at scale. Raphe mPhibr is the only startup that has shipped in combat and landed the big MoD order.

name: Ocius Technology
totalRaised: A$176M Australian Program of Record contract (March 2026)
description: Wind- and solar-powered Bluebottle autonomous surface vessels — won A$176M Program of Record March 2026 for 40 Bluebottles over 5 years for the Royal Australian Navy.
insight: Persistent USVs change the Pacific surveillance map. Ocius has survived 20m waves — proof of seaworthiness.

name: Anysphere
description: Building Cursor, the AI-powered code editor that is transforming software development. $29.3B valuation. $1B+ ARR. 1M+ daily active users. AI-first IDE that writes, edits, and debugs code with develop
insight: Cursor has achieved what GitHub Copilot couldn't — making AI the primary driver of code creation, not just a suggestion engine. At $29.3B and $1B+ ARR with 9,900% YoY growth, it's the fastest-growing developer tool in history.

name: Infleqtion
description: Develops atom-based quantum sensors including precision atomic clocks (100x more accurate than legacy systems), inertial navigation, and RF receivers for GPS-denied environments.

name: Quantum Art
description: Trapped-ion quantum computer using optical multiplexing to scale qubits — $100M Series A led by Bedford Ridge.
insight: Israel is quietly a top-5 quantum nation. Quantum Art has the biggest single round and the clearest hardware path.

name: Aleph Farms
description: Cultivated beef steaks grown from cow cells — world's first regulatory approval for cultivated beef (Israel MoH, December 2023). Facing significant challenges: 30% layoffs June 2024, emergency $25M ra
insight: Aleph won the first regulatory green light for cultivated beef. That's a first-mover moat in every market that opens next.

name: Apptronik
description: Commercial humanoid robotics (Apollo) for logistics and manufacturing — Series A $350M February 2025 led by B Capital/Capital Factory; total $425M+.

name: d-Matrix
description: In-memory computing chiplets for AI inference at the edge. Novel architecture that processes AI models where the data lives, eliminating bottlenecks.

name: Rain AI
description: Neuromorphic compute-in-memory AI chips — raising additional funding through 2025 with Sam Altman as lead investor. William Passo current CEO (Gordon Wilson stepped back).

name: Axelera AI
description: In-memory-compute AI accelerator chips (Metis AIPU) for computer vision at the edge — $68M Series B and €61.6M EU Chips JU grant.
insight: Edge AI is exploding and GPUs can't serve it. Axelera's analog IMC is the leading non-US alternative.

name: Telexistence
founder: Jin Tomioka, Ken Tomoyose, Susumu Tachi
description: VLA-powered humanoid (Astra) for retail labor — Seven-Eleven Japan partnership to deploy by 2029.
insight: Japan's labor crunch makes it the world's best humanoid market. Telexistence has the cleanest go-to-market there.

name: Helical Fusion
description: Stellarator fusion using HTS coils — Series A $15.6M (July 2025) plus $5.5M extension (Dec 2025); signed Japan's first fusion PPA with Aoki Super.
insight: Stellarators beat tokamaks on stability but lose on magnet cost. HTS fixes that — Helical is Japan's flagship bet.

name: EX-Fusion
description: Laser inertial-confinement fusion — Osaka University spinout demonstrating precision laser targeting; Series A ¥2.6B June 2025 plus JX Advanced Metals extension October 2025.
insight: NIF proved ignition. The next step is high-repetition-rate lasers — EX-Fusion has the Osaka University heritage and JX funding.

name: Speedata
description: Purpose-built APU (Analytics Processing Unit) for big-data workloads — $44M round backed by Intel CEO.
insight: Everyone focused on AI compute; Speedata targets the much larger analytics workloads — a structural blind spot.

name: Teramount
description: Self-aligning fiber-to-chip optical connectors for co-packaged optics in AI data centers — $50M round 2025.
insight: Copackaged optics is Nvidia's next bottleneck. Teramount makes the passive alignment step manufacturable.

name: Monumo
description: AI-designed electric motors optimising for efficiency, weight, and cost — ~$100M raised for next-generation industrial and automotive EV motors.
insight: Every EV, pump, and HVAC system needs better motors. Monumo's AI design is generating IP no human team can match.

name: DustPhotonics
description: 800G/1.6T co-packaged optics silicon for AI data centers — Series C $75M+ with MediaTek lead and Tier-1 hyperscaler design wins.
insight: AI cluster bandwidth is the next bottleneck. DustPhotonics is a rare non-US alternative with hyperscaler design wins.

name: Vention
description: Manufacturing automation platform with modular robotics — $95M Series C 2024 (Georgian-led) deploying across North American factories.
insight: SMB manufacturers can't hire robotics integrators. Vention's self-serve platform collapses the timeline.

name: Lightelligence
description: Photonic computing chips with optical matrix multiplication — $270M+ raised for Hummingbird photonic arithmetic chip targeting AI inference.
insight: Photonic compute is high-risk high-reward. Lightelligence has the most funded commercial bet in the category.

name: Thea Energy
description: Planar-coil stellarator fusion using HTS magnets — $37M+ Series A plus $20M DOE milestone award; pivoting to Eos fusion pilot plant in mid-2020s.
insight: Stellarators beat tokamaks on stability. Thea's planar-coil approach could win on manufacturing cost.

name: Realta Fusion
description: Mirror-machine fusion using HTS magnets — U. Wisconsin spinout, DOE milestone awardee; targeting industrial-heat fusion first.
insight: Industrial heat is a lower bar than grid electricity. Realta targets it first, betting mirror fusion gets there cheaper.

name: Elementl Power
description: Nuclear project developer for utilities — $50M+ raised; announced Google 1.8 GW nuclear deal 2025.
insight: Hyperscalers need nuclear and can't build it themselves. Elementl is the first developer they picked.

name: Orbital Composites
description: Robotic 3D printing systems for large, high-performance carbon fiber composite parts. Claims 100x faster production at 30%+ lower cost with 75% less labor. Working with Boeing, Lockheed Martin, and Ax
insight: Primarily funded through SBIR/STTR government contracts — the ultimate validation. Developing radiation-shielded cubesats at 10-50x lower cost. Their automated composite manufacturing addresses one of aerospace's biggest supply chain bottlenecks.

name: Astranis
description: Small geostationary satellites that deliver affordable internet to underserved regions. MicroGEO satellites where fiber doesn't reach.
insight: Astranis's MicroGEO approach is clever — GEO satellites where Starlink's LEO constellation can't economically serve. Dedicated bandwidth for specific countries and regions is a defensible niche.

name: Watershed
founder: Taylor Francis, Christian Anderson, Avi Itskovich
description: Enterprise carbon management platform helping companies measure, reduce, remove, and report their carbon footprint.

name: Cellares
founder: Fabian Gerlinghaus, Alex Kolicich, Omar Kurdi, Franziska Blaeschke
description: Automated cell therapy manufacturing platform to scale production of CAR-T and other cell therapies.

name: Ulysses Robotics
founder: Akhil Voorakkara, Jamie Wedderburn, Colm O'Brien, Will O'Brien
description: Underwater autonomous vehicles (AUVs) for seagrass restoration, Navy applications, and Great Barrier Reef operations — $38M Series A led by a16z American Dynamism.
insight: Autonomous subsea vehicles purpose-built for contested ocean environments. The team is tackling the hardest domain in autonomy — GPS-denied, comms-limited, high-pressure — which is exactly where defense customers need it most.

name: Nyobolt
description: Niobium-anode ultra-fast-charging batteries capable of 10-80% charge in under 5 minutes, with $150M contracted backlog.
insight: 5-minute charging removes the last psychological barrier to EV mass adoption. Nyobolt's the rare startup with Nobel-laureate chemistry + contracts.

name: Hystar
description: Thin-membrane PEM electrolysers with 2x throughput per m² of membrane, with contracts from Yara and Linde.
insight: Green hydrogen only scales if electrolysers halve in cost. Hystar's 2x throughput is the most credible path.

name: QunaSys
description: Quantum chemistry algorithms and software for materials and pharma — JPY 1.7B Series B2 late 2024.
insight: Japan's industrial giants want quantum for materials discovery. QunaSys is the native-language software provider.

name: Classiq
founder: Nir Minerbi, Amir Naveh, Yehuda Naveh
description: Quantum algorithm synthesis and compilation platform — ~$30M up-round November 2025 with AMD Ventures, Qualcomm, IonQ.
insight: Every quantum hardware needs software. Classiq is positioning as the hardware-agnostic compiler layer.

name: Neustark
description: Permanent CO2 mineralization in recycled concrete — $70M raised, operating plants and 1M-tonne target by 2030.
insight: Low-cost durable CO2 sequestration is the bottleneck. Neustark's concrete pathway is the cleanest scale play.

name: Rondo Energy
description: Industrial thermal batteries with 97% round-trip efficiency — 100 MWh+ deployments in CA oil fields (Holmes Western) and Thailand (SCG cement); $150M+ raised.
insight: Industrial heat is 20% of global emissions. Rondo is the only company shipping thermal batteries at commercial scale.

name: Porotech
founder: Tongtong Zhu, Yingjun Liu, Rachel Oliver
description: Porous-GaN platform enabling efficient red and full-color micro-LEDs for AR/VR displays and quantum sensing.
insight: Apple Vision Pro et al. can't scale without efficient micro-LEDs. Porotech's porous-GaN is one of the few solutions working.

name: Isembard
description: Franchised AI-run precision-machining micro-factories (MasonOS) for aerospace, defense, and robotics components.
insight: The West is out of machinists. Isembard's franchised AI factories create capacity without training an entire generation.

name: Perigee Aerospace
description: Small-satellite dedicated launch vehicle (Blue Whale) — contracts signed with Sweden's Esrange spaceport and the Philippine Space Agency.
insight: Regional spaceports need regional launchers. Perigee is the rare small-launch startup with international anchor contracts.

name: Hailo
founder: Orr Danon, Hadar Zeitlin, Avi Baum
description: Edge AI accelerator chips — Hailo-10H shipping as the first on-device generative-AI edge chip.
insight: Hailo is one of two serious edge-AI chip winners globally. On-device genAI at Hailo-10H margins will print money.

name: NordSpace
description: Canadian orbital launch vehicle and spaceport operator — $38M+ with maiden suborbital launch planned winter 2026.
insight: Canada has Kepler, MDA, and Telesat but no sovereign launch. NordSpace fills that gap with both rocket and pad.

name: Corintis
founder: Remco van Erp, Sam Harrison, Elison Matioli
description: Silicon-based microfluidic chip cooling extracting 10x more heat than conventional methods — $58M Series A with Microsoft endorsement.
insight: AI chips are now thermally constrained, not transistor-constrained. Corintis is the clearest Microsoft-endorsed fix.

name: Asterra
description: Satellite-based subsurface leak detection using SAR and L-band radar physics — $20M+ raised, deployed with ~1,000 utilities globally.
insight: Water infrastructure loses 30%+ to leaks globally. Asterra is the only satellite-data firm detecting them at continental scale.

name: Portal Space Systems
description: Rapidly maneuverable Supernova spacecraft with solar-thermal propulsion delivering 6 km/s delta-V — $70M+ total including $50M Series A late 2025 and $45M STRATFI.
insight: Space Force wants RPO-capable spacecraft with huge delta-V. Portal's solar-thermal bet delivers where electric propulsion can't.

name: Crusoe Energy
description: AI data center company using stranded energy sources. $10B+ valuation. Started mining bitcoin with flare gas, now managing the largest AI data center buildout including the $500B Stargate project. Bac
insight: Crusoe's pivot from Bitcoin mining to AI data centers was prescient. Managing Stargate's $500B buildout positions them at the center of AI infrastructure — the picks-and-shovels play of the AI era. Projected $2B revenue in 2026.

name: Field AI
description: General-purpose embodied AI foundation models for robots in complex industrial environments. $405M raised at $2B valuation. SoCal's own robotics lab to rival Bay Area.

name: Retro Biosciences
description: Targeting three core aging 'bugs': toxic blood molecules, genetic errors, and cellular cleanup. RTR-242 drug targets autophagy. Sam Altman invested $180M.

name: Agility Robotics
description: Building Digit, a bipedal robot designed for warehouse logistics. The first humanoid robot deployed in real commercial operations. Amazon testing partnership.

name: Hive AI
description: AI company providing machine learning models via APIs for automated content moderation, contextual advertising, and visual recognition for enterprises.

name: QuEra Computing
founder: Mikhail Lukin, Vladan Vuletic, Markus Greiner
description: Neutral-atom quantum computer using programmable arrays of Rydberg atoms. Harvard/MIT spinout pushing the boundaries of quantum processing.

name: Pulsar Fusion
description: Nuclear-fusion rocket engine (Sunbird) for interplanetary propulsion — demonstrated live plasma ignition in March 2026.
insight: The only plausible path to human Mars colonization is fusion propulsion. Pulsar's live plasma is the first serious milestone.

name: Zama
description: Fully homomorphic encryption libraries and compilers enabling computation on encrypted data — $57M Series B in June 2025.
insight: Regulated AI will require training on encrypted data. Zama is the leader in FHE — a category once thought impractical.

name: Genespire
description: Off-the-shelf in-vivo gene therapies via Immune-Shielded Lentiviral Vector platform — $52M Series B led by Sofinnova.
insight: In-vivo gene therapy at scale requires immune-shield tech. Genespire's platform licensed from San Raffaele is a rare asset.

name: QuamCore
description: Superconducting-qubit fault-tolerant architecture targeting million-qubit systems — $26M Series A 2025.
insight: Fault tolerance is the endgame. QuamCore's architecture could leapfrog incumbents if the physics works.

name: Quantum Motion
description: Silicon-based quantum computing using proprietary quantum dot architecture — ~$60M raised with UK National Quantum Computing Centre partnership.
insight: Silicon-qubit quantum is the scalability bet. UCL + Oxford pedigree gives Quantum Motion a credible shot.

name: Causaly
description: Scientific literature AI for biomedical research — $95M raised (ICONIQ-led Series B) with major pharma deployments.
insight: Pharma R&D is bottlenecked on literature search. Causaly's Causely platform is the clearest automation of the analyst layer.

name: Black Forest Labs
founder: Robin Rombach, Andreas Blattmann, Patrick Esser
description: Creators of FLUX open-weight image generation models — leading European foundation-model lab with $200M+ raised at $1B+ valuation.
insight: FLUX already beats Midjourney on several benchmarks. Black Forest Labs is the new European AI champion post-Stability collapse.

name: Dust
description: Enterprise AI agents platform — ex-OpenAI team with ~$70M+ raised and customers including Alan, Qonto, and PayFit.
insight: Enterprise AI agents are the next billion-dollar layer. Dust's ex-OpenAI team is the strongest European contender.

name: Photoroom
description: On-device generative imaging AI with tens of millions of users — Series B $43M at $500M valuation December 2024.
insight: On-device AI wins the long-tail prosumer market. Photoroom has one of the only apps with millions of paying users.

name: MakinaRocks
description: Industrial MLOps platform for heavy industry — $40M Series B, Korean conglomerate deployments.
insight: Korean chaebols are the world's hardest enterprise AI customers. MakinaRocks is the incumbent vendor.

name: World Labs
founder: Fei-Fei Li, Justin Johnson, Christoph Lassner, Ben Mildenhall
description: Spatial intelligence / Large World Models (LWMs) reasoning about 3D — Series B $1B+ February 2026 at $5B+ valuation following Marble world-generation product launch November 2025.
insight: Language models will hit limits. World models — 3D, physics, causality — are the next frontier. Fei-Fei Li started the field.

name: Chroma Medicine
description: Epigenetic editing (non-DNA-cutting) platform from Flagship Pioneering — $260M+ raised; leading the epigenome programming field; merged with nChroma Bio 2024.
insight: Epigenetic editing may prove safer and more scalable than CRISPR. Chroma is the Flagship-backed category leader.
